reviews.review_text: A good example of how to make a good remake, the terror of the original game was not lost and I can definitely recommend this game.

As a Dead Space veteran and aficionado, I went into this game hoping for a faithful recreation of one of my favourite experience of all time with updated graphics and polished gameplay. I had expected that my muscle memory for this game would allow me to casually run through it while appreciating the new visuals.

Boy was I wrong. In the BEST way. 

The story, progression, and map layout are mostly the same, but with many MEANINGFUL changes big and small. The core experience remains the same as the 2008 original, but new challenges, enemy placements, story elements, and map design made it an entirely fresh experience for me. Things are similar enough that the game felt familiar, but different enough that I once again felt alone, isolated, and anxious about entering the next room. Like I was playing my favourite game for the first time again, 18 years later. I have to say this is the perfect remake. I'm incredibly thankful for the creative and design teams deciding to shake things up a bit and not just slap a new coat of paint on an already-amazing masterpiece.

The game still has some bugs, three years out of the box. I've experienced some audio glitches, one that actually locked-up the game when it transitioned into a cutscene. A couple of times I've had an enemy get stuck in a wall. The game also runs like garbage on Linux but does very well on Windows. I'm sure there are some tweaks I could have done to make the Linux experience better, but I have dualboot set-up so I don't have to.

Only thing I'm actually sad about is they didn't have Keith Szarabajka reprise his role as the VA for Terrence Kyne. That guy's voice is iconic. :(

Altogether, 10/10 experience. Convergence is at hand. We shall all be made whole.

Use to run amazing on my PC.  I'm guessing with the routine Windows and Nvidia Driver updates, the game's performance has become borked and requires an immediate update to patch certain performance issues such as traversal stuttering and massive framerate drops. 

I run on a 3070 and at the launch of Dead Space I was running the game on 2k resolution and would average 79-90 fps, even higher in the intro sequence prior to the first chapter of the game.  In the same intro sequence a couple years later, I am now dipping in the 40-60fps range,  and can barely peak to even 70fps.  The game needs a massive performance overhaul for me to seriously recommend this game to a friend on PC. 

Hard pass at the moment.

Dead Space Remake is one of, if not, my favourite horror games ever and likely will always be.

I initially finished it a few times on Xbox before deciding to move to the Steam version, were it plays even better with a mouse and keyboard. 
It's a game I can come back to for no other reason that the gameplay is so good.

It's a shame EA decided to park the franchise after such a great remake.

I thought the original Dead Space was better than RE4 way back when they launched and years later I think Dead Space Remake is better that RE4 Remake (they're both excellent).
Poorly optimized and  game breaking bugs. Do not recommend

A classic story driven horror game.
This game is a very good example of how to make a good remake, they changed a few things and I think for the best + the game did not lose the terror of the original one.
The price tag on this game is crazy high I would say get it only on an omega sale, the main story + side missions should take you around of 12 ~ 15 hours of game play and if you go for 100% probably 25 hours.

The Good:
- Atmosphere: The silence and tension keep you on your toes.
- Combat is well done in my opinion, you don't only rely on your weapons but on your abillities to use the enviorment against foes.
- Story is great, I have a few niche problems or things I would do otherwise but the overview is good.
- Beautiful enviorment, especially the space.
- Zero gravity feels very much realstic, not that any of us have experience but the confusion or lose of YX axis is how one would imagine it.

The Bad:
- The game relies on the old school jump scares, while some of them are fine but too many for my taste.
- The remake posses a few bugs I've noticed. The biggest one is the menu bug where it has some sort of a render issue, it is not game breaking but you notice the framerate change.
- The price tag is crazy, I know it is a remake but still... I guess classic EA greed.

Extremely late to the party at this point but the game is totally worth it. 

Sound design is excellent, Weapons in this game carry that strong ' oomph ' when fired which is a must when your game revolves around shooting. 

I love the addition of side quests. Exploration is rewarding without feeling dragged out, with some minor exceptions for achievement hunting. But even then it's tame enough. 

I've never had issues with performance, stable FPS all throughout. But I have had stutters in-game when loading between different sectors of the map. But that was understandable given how this game never had a loading screen during gameplay. With the sole exception of starting up the game. 

Game good, play it.

Having played the original release on PS3, my memory was fuzzy about this game, but it quickly came back to me. All the same horror vibes, the great feeling running around, the sense of hoplessness. All great. It runs and looks really good too, if a little stuttery when first getting into new areas. My main issue has been a lack of ammo. Only carry a few weapons and you're guaranteed to pick up just their ammo type. And remember to use monsters own chopped off limbs against them, they can instakill.

Like the original with an expanded story and better graphics, although i prefer the original games atmosphere and if your pc is pre-2016 or below a gtx 1050 in terms of power i would recommend the original game

i need Daddy Clark to breed me. 

Im glad this wasn't a first person game because I'd definely have peed my pamts playing it. Dead Space is in league of its own when it comes to atmospheric horror, with some brutal ways to kill stuff thrown in.

This is exactly a remake masterpiece.
You don't even need to play the og cause this is completely superior to the og.

hopefully I'm getting a ds2 remastered before my funeral
Absolutely amazing remake. I hope one day they remake Dead Space 2. They remade this game without ruining the story of the original. This is a solid 10/10

Play the original or Dead Space 2 instead. I only paid $11 for this game so I'm not that mad about the following issues.

But this game has been out for 3 years and the graphics options are still bugged if you use DLSS or FSR all the textures become the super blurry. Fine, run it with TAA instead, the game runs on a Steam Deck pretty well... but the stutter.

But this has to be the best example of what NOT to do for game devs when it comes to shader compilation. The shader stutter is CONTSTANT every time a new texture or effect is on screen and the game will pause for 4-20 SECONDS between each frame while things load in. It doesn't matter if your running on Windows, Linux, or the steam deck. It would be one thing if there were a couple 300 ms stutters every now and then, or a big freeze when moving between levels, but no the stutters are constantly interrupting traversal, combat, cutscenes, everything. Any amount of spooky atmosphere and space horror this game may have had is completely nullified by the shader compilation stutter.

It's crazy this game is still allowed to be sold.
I'm so glad to finally be done so I can uninstall it.

This game is horribly optimized for PC. This game suffers from a rendering bug which causes the framerates to tank whenever you open a menu. They still have not fixed this problem after all this time of the game being out. This is infuriating, because my PC is supposed to run this game fine, but with this bug in the game, it's pretty much unplayable for me. Very frustrating and disappointing.

Too much action. No suspense or horror elements get to shine through, because it's an action fest. Every room, every corridor is full of enemies. There is no break to breathe, so if you're into that you'll enjoy it. For me though it's just overload, and it makes the game kind of monotonous. It's literally just combat, one area to the next. Nothing else really matters.

Bro they drop a good remake and then said lets stop remaking the second. EA whats up with yall. first it was battle front two with no updates now its this. Truly tragic. If money the problem lower the sale of this remake pull those funds and make a second. Tf is the problem

Great remake. Preserved the original atmosphere, vision, and spirit of the game. No bugs, no tech issues, no weird glitches. Very spooky atmosphere, environment, level design, audio. Weapons are satisfying, exploring/collecting items is fun, story progresses nicely. Love a little space horror. Genre-defining game here.

So far this is the best horror game I've ever played, the graphics are amazing, and the sound and world are so very unsettling. It remind me of the movie Event Horizon. I'm only a little ways into the game, and it truly scares me to my core. It's beautifully made, and when I finally finish it I want to try Alien Isolation next. Great job Motive, for remaking an old game and making it look amazing and you got me interested enough to buy it with the modern graphics.

Great game for my first time playing it! 

Looks visual stunning with all the bells like HDR, 49inch curved, 2k 240hz on a 4080RTX super 16GB with 64 ram on a i9-4900k, oh and scary azz hell! lol

originally i had snaged this game on sale,and at the beginning i was a bit sceptical because, recently learning it was made by EA, i thought<<EA,making a good game how is that even possible>> and so i was cautiously optimistic about playing this game.And after beating it, i can safely say it has proved me wrong.
this game had my heart rate up on more than one occasion, especially at the hive mind fight at the end. and like games such as breath of the wild, you can upgrade your gear and weapons as you go on,however unlike BOTW,even with all that OP gear you still seem to be somewhat at a disadvantage because you're to busy paniking franticaly trying to kill the necromorphs.Also,isacc clarke is such a  likeable and relatable character.
I will say i did play this game on my steam deck and it was a bit rough at the start even on low settings because it is another case of poor optimisaton, but lossless scaling did improve it slighty and only really dipped in areas with lots of entities.but besides that,i can say this game is a masterclass and a must play on my part!

Deadspace.

One of those horror games that you really cant forget. 
The enemies are designed to be gross, the environment mimics a very claustrophobic nightmare mixed with fleshy biological hazards that echo the voices of the forsakened. 

The gunplay feels extremely smooth and the enemies actually feel each shot instead of a clunky animation, like they have actual physics to it. 

And the story is great, not the greatest but interesting enough to actually care about the characters. 

Overall this game is a wonderful remake, its truly a shame that this franchise most likely will not get a continuation of it
this game somehow has more glitches that ruin the gameplay than the original. EA can chug my ♥♥♥
Great remake! Had so much fun going back and playing this. It's on the short side but I am for sure going back and playing new game plus and upgrading all the guns.
Im get abducted by aliens, Mary dosen't matter now. James Sunderland Aprove this game.
Um waiter there seems to be something wrong with my NBA 2K game, there are necromorphs aliens in it.

Before I played Dead Space, I didn't even like horror games and wasn't the biggest fan, I'm more akin to 'casual' games I guess and found games like this too daunting. Regardless, I picked up Dead Space whilst it was on sale this April and was blown away. This game is just immaculate man.  I remember when I first played, I kinda struggled with the enemies and rules and died frequently but nevertheless pushed on. Now I'm 40 hours in, have completed all the achievements (except Impossible mode trophy), fully upgraded all weapons and equipment and done multiple NG+ playthroughs with different weapons and I RARELY replay games at all. And the game is still leaving me with wanting more.

This game had an unexpected but welcome affect on me. Combat is sublime and makes you feel like a Force-wielding Space Marine from hell, sound design and audio is incredible, weapons are more unique than other action-horror titles and pack a huge punch (love the Contact Beam and Flamethrower especially!). Story is entertaining. This is one of those games where you just want to beat it again and again and do everything because it's so polished.

I urge you to pick this game up and just try it for yourself, worth your time. 9/10

Now if you'll excuse me, I'm gonna go and try to beat the game in Impossible mode, then maybe play Dead Space 2 as I've heard lots of good things about it!

Screw You EA for putting an incredible franchise like this on ice.

Edit: Just came to say a couple of days after this review posted I beat the game on Impossible and got the 100% achievement. Man that was incredible
Really top quality remaster this. Played the original many years ago and this really is a great game. Wish they would remaster the other 2 games too.
Great remake of the greatest horror game of all time. Even performance is good.
Great game, very well remade! Loved the original and the remake is a welcome update!

This game single hanledly showed me just how ♥♥♥♥♥♥ up the mind of one man can be. I am still so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ traumatised from the jumpscares contained in this game, and i still cannot find a game that can give me the same amount of fear, trauma and love more than the Dead Space trilogy, but it's a shame that this perfect game got in thehands of the Devil (aka EA). My favorite game in the whole trilogy is by far and hands down Dead Space 2, but i also really love the remaster and the third game, although i cannot find another person to play with. I reccomend this game with every fiber in my body, i think atleast every gamer HAS to experience Dead Space. 10/10

its such a beautiful game i think its fun i play as this guy named isacc and hes like "hey hammond here" and then he gotta fix this boat called the usg ishamura because somebody forgot to turn it off then this annoying bich woman nicole keeps facetiming me every 3 minutes talking about "isaac make us whole" like im literally busy i will fill you up later then this foid named kendra keeps arguing with hamood  and nobody can agree on anything except dying there are negromorphs everywhere but isaac got the worlds strongest boot so its okay my favorite part is when i put the big red marker back because everybody was getting upset about it
graphics are very good
10/10 fixed the ship and somehow made everything worse

I found this game after the remaster came out and never thought to play it. I should have gotten it sooner and played the OG version. Anyone that loves the third person horror genre will love this. the RE franchise and this are amazingly similar with immersive environments and combat mechanics. 5 stars.

El horror no es mi género, pero este me gustó bastante. Me recordó a Resident Evil 4, pero con mucha mejor ambientación. El sonido del juego te mantiene en tensión para mi y el combate, que está muy bueno. Me gusta cuando te permiten defenderte y, aun así, te puedes estar cagando de miedo.
Muito bom o jogo as mecanicas de stasis e kinetic sao mt legais, o jogo é lindo e mt bem feito

An amazing game and an amazing remake, but performance leaves a lot to be desired.  I maintain a stable 60fps most of the time (with dips down to 50) at DLSS performance at 1440p.  That's not the worst thing in the world, but the game also suffers regularly from stutters when it loads areas as you move through them.  It also has a horrific DLSS/FSR implementation, the worst I have ever seen.  VRS is forced on when you use DLSS/FSR, even if you have the VRS toggle off, which makes every texture in the game look horribly muddy.  I had to download Nvidia's Profile Inspector to manually adjust the game's DLSS implementation to eliminate VRS.  I have never had that issue with any other game using DLSS.  On a related note, the game has a version of DLSS that has terrible ghosting, and it's a far easier fix, just download the latest DLSS version from Nvidia and drop it into the game files, replacing the old DLSS.

It is a shame EA held the game to frankly unreasonable expectations, expecting 5 million copies from a horror game that hasn't been in the cultural zeitgeist for the past decade.  DSR sold 2 million in 6 months, however the original Dead Space sold 1 million in 4 months.  By all accounts, the remake sold better than the original, yet it wasn't enough for EA.  Dead Space 2 Remake wasn't greenlighted because it failed to meet those sales expectations.  Mind you, horror games do not have massive appeal, and don't sell as well as say shooters.  Despite that it still outsold the original game, and was extremely well received by the public.  I can only hope that after Motive finishes up with their Iron Man game, they can convince the higher ups to let them do a Dead Space 2 Remake, hopefully overhaul the third with a remake, and move on to Dead Space 4.

Great game, has a lot of similarities to Prey 2017 (Yes I know it's a remake of the 2008 game)
Total playtime was about 12 hours without rushing. I quit the game after the first couple of hours an returned to finish it few months later, learned to love the game
GORGEOUS game with OUTSTANDING audio. Loved the first game and the remaster hits the spot, but a LOT better now with modern day tech
The game was a lot of fun. I feel like they still kept the same feel of the original game with way better graphics. A good remake in general.

Amazing remake, after some tinkering.
The game is very good but the absense of graphical options made it unplayable for me at first.
No FOV slider, i get motion sickness from low FOV, fixed with cheat engine.
Forced TAA, i wear glasses to see things sharp and clear, TAA is like i'm not wearing my glasses, was able to disable TAA with a mod.
After these changes i really enjoyed playing through this game.

True to the original but some things were changed like story elements and gameplay mechanics, to me these were all improvements.
The graphics are the biggest improvement obviously, even though in my memory this is what the original looked like.
Runs very smooth and i encountered no bugs or crashes.

Hopefully they'll remake the second game as well!

Genuinely an amazing game! It's got some changes, but they almost always add to the experience. There's almost nothing bad I can say about the game except for the terrible performance. There are a couple of guides published/uploaded that fix these issues to some extent. Look up 'Dead Space 2023 cache fix', this is what helped me. There are still some stutters and occasionally some really big ones, but, it's 80-90% better now. 
I really hope EA treats Dead Space 2 with equal respect whenever they decide to remake it.

What a beautiful remaster/remake. This game does everything right. It stays true to the originals experience, making sure that the gameplay and atmosphere are both familiar and improved. Proper update to modern mechanics without changing what made the game great in the first place. It truly builds on the store with full voice acting and side missions which flesh out the lore of what happened to the ishimura. This is how you bring back a 10 year old game.

Absolutely amazing remake of the original game. Motive was really cooking here. If you loved OG Dead Space and haven't played in a while this is a perfect way to re-experience that original 2008 horror. It's beautiful. I wish all remakes were this good.

An awesome update to a great game. Visuals look awesome, game runs smoothly. Highly recommended if you are on the fence. It also often goes on sale for $15 or less and that's a perfect time to grab it.
The Game has great story content, The remake was perfect, even better than the original, Impossible run is not that hard, but an good challenge.
Wonderful game. Some hitching here and there despite my higher end PC (4080 super, 7900x) running settings cranked at 4k.
Such a great story, missions and atmosphere. Peak

Prefacing this by saying I liked it. Shooter Elements are satisfying, Horror Story was interesting. UI is clean af. But as a Horror Game I think it fails, because after a strong start the game inundates you with weapons and the whole Ishimura is riddled with ambushes/jump scares, so it just becomes expected. Shootouts in nearly every room just don't help building up tension. After the game makes you turn off the light to power other stuff near the beginning, I am a little surprised I did not have to do that over and over... But the game already replaces the good lighting with emergency lights during it's many Biohazard-Quarantine-Kill-Everything-We-Throw-At-You-In-This-Room Segments, so still counts in my book. And the longer the game went on the more annoyed I became with this, as it did evoke an "Oh god, it's dark and there are monsters" feeling but more a "Okay, where are they hiding/coming from"
Since I am an enormous coward this Not-Really-A-Horror-Game allowed me to finish and enjoy the game (3rd person-perspective might be helping too). The last chapter had me pretty annoyed with the leadup to the finale, felt a little long and uninteresting... Still overall recommend looking into it, I think it deserves its status as a classic.
Kewl Game. Story could get vague later on specifically about the origin of stuff. In other words, story will get metal gear like.

Great remake. Really great game with good mechanics and combat. Seriously buy it and play it. Side quests are super fun too. I love this game. Love what they did with the story, characters, and plot. 8/10.


Some complaints that I think would make the game perfect. Stasis and telekenisis are overpowered. To balance this, stasis recharge stations should only give you one use stasis refill, not a complete recharge. Telekenisis should be upgraded in order to kill, not have it right off the bat. 

Ammo is plentiful, almost every time an enemy will drop ammo for you. I think the game should provide you with half of ammo drops it usually does. I play on hardest difficulty and found it too easy with all the ammo drops.

There were times enemies spawned in but I didn't hear it because the map was too loud. I actually loved this. Got me good a couple times.

I wish Isaac's footsteps and audio were louder and clunkier, like how Cronos did it. Helps a lot with immersion. 

Brutes are really easy to kill. Like one stasis, run behind the back, and kill it. No real sense of dread or  tension. If stasis is nerfed like mentioned above, I think it would help.

I also wish that enemies played dead much more. That felt that was pretty common back in the original so I was sad to see enemies drop and drop an item, knowing with certainty an enemy was dead all the time. In fact, enemies almost always drop an item. I hate that. Should be much more sparing. 

Quick time events when an enemy grabs you should be a different button/key everytime to really enhance the dreadful aspect of being in the grasp of a necromorph. 

Finally, the game is just too easy as is. I think with these changes, as a new difficulty, it could actually be hard as intended. 

All of these fixes would make the game a 10/10 for me. Still, it's a great action horror that everyone should play.

9/10
Minus a point because:
- Unskippable cutscenes.
- Some fights are too cluttered.
- A couple of parts in the game become a bit tiring.
- Cliches in the story, despite it being better than most video game stories.
This game might have the best writing of any mainstream AAA survival Horror game.

Very good game. But it is not very good optimized, I have lag and stutter on some places, yes I play on max but with my hardware it is strange to have so low drops. Even with upscaling there is some lag.
Great game. One of the best zombie shoter. Do not understand why they call it a horror

A great story, the visuals/graphics are modern and the sound was creepy and helped give you that feeling of dread. It's too bad they decided not to continue with these remakes I think that is a wrong move.

Great survival horror game, and the small halls of the spaceship help increase the oppressive feeling of the atmosphere. The monsters are absolutely terrifying and the combat is super satisfying. Cutting limbs off and blasting skin away is gruesomely awesome and makes me look forward to combat just as much as I fear it.

+ Amazing, just amazing horror game, especially if you have an OLED monitor

+ I've played countless game on my OLED monitor & they all look amazing, but Dead Space really made me go wow & just actually managed to amaze me with how great it looked & how great the horror atmosphere was

+ Very high quality game

+ Good gunplay, good mechanics

Cons:
- Dated console controls when using computer consoles/inventories, if we are on PC just let us use the damn mouse, you could die if you get attacked while having to take your hands off the movement keys to press the arrow keys to interact
The standard for any survival horror game to follow. Nobody has ever done it better.

Its pretty good, in my opinion this game didn't [i]need[/i] a remake, other than maybe some fixes to get it running better on modern systems. But nonetheless the remake is pretty good, they added and changed enough that its not just a graphical overhaul but not so much that it feels radically different from the original. Most of the additions and changes are for the better.

Some people don't like that they gave Isaac a voice but i think it was the right move. It adds character and his voice work isn't exaggerated, he doesn't quip one-liners or talk to himself or complain dramatically about his situation. It makes his character feel professional, and a lot more proactive. Instead of being told what to do every chapter its more like something breaks and people freak out, then Isaac pipes up that he can probably fix things. its a small change but i like it.

I did have a few stutters early on but those went away after fiddling with the graphics a bit, and this isn't a major issue but story cutscenes & dialogue aren't skippable, which gets a bit annoying on repeat playthroughs.

It does lead to some unintentionally funny moments where something is horrifying on your first playthrough, then on the second its designated phone time.
its just great the updated graphics make the game a lot better
If you like games such as Resident Evil, do yourself a favor and come be introduced to this wonderful franchise. Remember: Space is Dead because EA won't stop killing it!

Wow what a game - just finished it. The story is so well done, and the graphics hold up as outstanding even in 2026. The difficulty for me was perfectly tuned at the default settings. Puzzles were just the right level of difficulty - I maybe looked one up once, but other than that just figured them out for myself. Highly recommended. Catch it on sale though!

I don’t play many horror games; they scare me too much, and I don’t enjoy that. The only games of the genre I tend to like are those that give you guns to shoot the horrifying creatures with. This game had that, and I heard very good things about the original series and this remake. I really enjoyed this game. Art direction and exploration are stellar. I love unsettling creature design and body horror, and semi-linear games are my favorite kind. I also adored how all the UI was in-universe (your inventory and map are holograms, your health is a light bar on your back, etc.). Really recommend this game to anyone who enjoys those aspects of a work, 8/10.
The only technical issue I came across was just general low performance (I was typically getting 25-40 fps, usually resting in the 30s), which is due to my not-so-super computer hardware, and I’ve read from many sources that this game is overly CPU-intensive.
ART or COOL rating: ART

Fans of the Alien franchise, The Thing, Event Horizon, and Resident Evil 4 will delight in this spectacular sci-fi horror remake of the classic original game.  The game is terrifying, yet the pacing, story, graphics, and visceral gameplay are so good and keep you returning to this dark and claustrophobic nightmare of grotesque dismemberment, hallucinations, and existential dread.  10/10

Completely unplayable due to shader compilation issues that appear to be baked directly into the engine. I spent four hours testing various ProtonDB launch commands, workarounds, and hacks, but there does not seem to be any reliable way to make the game stable.

It is a shame, because the game itself is gorgeous and the gameplay is phenomenal. In its current state, however, this should not be sold on Steam.

The first time I played this remake was on PS5, and it ran flawlessly.  No stutters, hiccups, nothing.  The PC version is a completely different story: traversal stutter, hitches, freezes, you name it.  It's completely unplayable and its really disappointing to see nothing has been done to fix it so it's at least playable.  Maybe Nvidia users will have a better experience, but this ran like absolute garbage on my machine.  Save your money and time and just play the OG for PC, and if you really want to play the remake, you're better off doing it on console.

Really, REALLY recommended,this is my favourite horror game of all time, because it doesnt rely on jumpscares to scare u, it relys on the atmosphere of the suroundings and makes u feel like u never safe anywhere,sometimes this game scares u mentally making u feel like isaac,compared this to the og 2008 theres a huge differences although i can say that the 2008 feel scarier than this but this one still great with everything improved from the og game, glad this game got remaked making the fandom back alive,but it seems EA hates money and will not expecting to remaked 2 and 3 sadly, maybe one day, i w ould choose this than any RE ngl(except biohazard and village).

I want to love this remake so badly. This game looks like it has a lot of really good things to offer. But every time I boot this game, this game is nothing but waiting for assets to load in all of the time to the point where I genuinely can't continue playing past Chapter 2. EA for the love of god, fix the rendering issues.
Incredibly enjoyable experience, especially with the modern visuals uplift.
I really enjoyed it. It was my first completed survival horror game! I hope to see more dead space

If you loved the original, then move right on through and pick this up as it is the definitive way to play this game now. Other than some minor things, this remake takes everything that was great about the first game and builds on it to improve it here. 

There are no more loading screens and the ship is now 'open' to explore instead of being sequestered into chapter sections allowing for seamless backtracking for supplies and secrets. The zero-g sections are now full flight sections like the sequel has instead of just jumping around awkwardly between walls. The terrible turret sections have been completely overhauled and are much less frustrating now. Many of the weapons have been overhauled to make them more useful and upgrading has been improved by removing 'empty' nodes and adding in special upgrades you can find or buy. 

Isaac now has his classic voice from the later titles and all of the dialogue has been rerecorded for the update. It's a bit of a mixed bag but overall I think it enhances the game. The visual upgrades are excellent and while others have reported bad issues with stuttering, I didn't really experience much of that. Running at 1440P, ultra, with DLSS set to quality I typically had ~70 fps.
It's a fantastic remake of a great game. However, the performance issues on PC have still not been fixed and probably never will be. If you can stand the stutters it's worth playing.

Great atmosphere and interesting storyline! Played on hard difficulty only and the game still felt more like an action horror instead of a survival horror, but it was a fun ride - using all weapons at their full potential in late game felt awesome (didn't start an ng+ playthrough though). Many thanks to the developers! It shows the game was made with love to the original :)
too shorttt for the price but the graphic is good and actractive ...jumpscare na ahh for me no jump scare just idsgusting things
An already incredible game, remastered to make it even more fun to play in modern times with incredible graphics and framerates. Shows what a marvel this game was when it first came out!

Mannn I loved this game. What a beautiful remaster from the original. The scares the graphics. everything feels so smooth and the game play is flawless. I would love if they did a remake of 2 and 3. I truly believe they could blow up. Especially Dead space 2. I cant say enough good things about this remake/Remaster.
I can’t believe that I have ignored this game for so long. Great atmosphere and very fun unique combat.

Good game with a slow start and annoying performance issues.
From the opening moments, you already know where Dead Space is heading: a corrupt company is entangled in a government cover-up, and you're caught in the middle. 

That doesn't stop it from sneaking in a couple of clever twists, though. Between the cult of Unitology, a conspiratorial government, and the conniving workers aboard the planet-cracker, you're not entirely sure who's responsible for the carnage until the end. 

The industrial setting of the Ishimura is perfect for Dead Space's scrappy gameplay. Much like BioShock, your greatest weapon is whatever you happen to find in the environment. A sharp pipe to throw, spare ammo for the weapon you don't use, or a tool that interacts well with the map layout, synergy with the environment runs high in later levels, upselling Isaac's resourcefulness as an engineer. However, at the start when all you have is a plasma cutter and a dream, scrounging around for resources can feel more like a nervous chore preventing you from progressing. Likewise, the manual save points, though typical for horror games to build suspense, can be an irritating hassle when combined with the frequent backtracking. 

It's a good remake of a good game, yet neither are perfect. The story is effective and interesting, yet fairly predictable. The gameplay is crisp, creepy, and punchy, yet it can get repetitive. The performance is almost paradoxical: theoretically, it runs well on a Steam Deck. Medium settings exceed "High" in most other games (the visual benefits of "High" in this title are negligible) and when shaders compile it runs at fairly high framerates (I had 120fps at 4k on a 7900xtx). Yet studders bring the good average frame rates to a standstill whenever you load into a new section, ESPECIALLY the first times you play. Over time, performance improves. But occasional crashes and framehitches still leave a bad taste in the mouth. 

Overall, I'd rate it 7/10: many high points and a consistently good experience, but enough lows throughout to prevent it from being entirely positive. Is it a "must play"? No. Perhaps for action-horror fans or someone who really loves the "Aliens"-esque industrial sci-fi aesthetic. Yet its a good game you certainly won't regret playing, and it's one you won't certainly won't forget. In short, I'd learn a little more about the Ishimura before you come aboard. But no spoilers!
the remake is so good, cranking everything up to the max settings and averaging around 180fps is amazing. the horror is there for sure, pick this game up when you get the chance, it is so good!!

Game is a superb remake. Haven't played the OG since launch which is great because I can barely remember the majority of it, making the game "new" for me again! Very fun game

Dead Space was already a classic, but this remake is the definitive way to experience it. The visual overhaul looks absolutely incredible. The USG Ishimura looks stunning, with a crazy amount of detail packed into every corridor and blood-soaked hallway. I found myself regularly stopping just to appreciate the environmental design and lighting.

The atmosphere is perfect too. Between the sound design, lighting, and constant feeling that something is lurking nearby, the game keeps the tension high the entire way through. It’s one of those games where just walking between objectives feels stressful, especially on higher difficulties. Definitely play this one on hard.

My only real criticism is that enemy spawns become a little predictable after a while. After the first few hours, you definitely start recognising the patterns, like which vent they’ll come from or how that necromorph lying on the floor that you conveniently 'forgot' to stomp is obviously a trap. That said, the game still managed to catch me completely off guard at times. One moment that genuinely got me was when a necromorph suddenly pulled me out of the store menu mid-purchase. I didn’t even know that could happen until it did!

In summary, the Dead Space Remake feels like exactly what a remake should be: faithful to the original while improving almost every aspect of it. In my view, it's easily one of the best survival horror games in years.
Game was amazing when first released and the remaster makes it even that much better. Just buy it already
Love the updated version. The ambiance & immersion is way better than the OG. Shame that DS2 and DS3 wouldn't get the same treatment.

This game was awesome, if you liked the original then you will like this one.

You might need to fiddle with the graphics settings a bit to get smooth FPS.
The reflections in the game are horrible... at the start of the game you can see it if you have graphics maxed out.
A lot of settings seem to do very little while taking a huge chunk of FPS, there is a great video about it from BenchmarKing on YT.
Also, using something like DLSS Swapper to switch to a newer DLSS version to remove the ghosting is a must!

There is also the thing about the game being blurry and has a green tint.
I removed them with Reshade, used the "Clarity and Sharpness - deblur - ReShade" from nexusmods and used LiftGammaGain to add more red and blue to fight against the green.

Sadly i read that there will not be a Dead Space 2 remake as Dead Space 1 did not sell enough.
But since DS2 is so great, i would suggest you try it out if you do not care about the graphics.

Never having played the originals, I definitely can't see what all the fuss is about. That being said, it's a good scifi shooter with some fairly mid horror elements. It's a linear story with a semi-open map that's not too large which makes full completion effortless but fun.

If you, like me, go in blind without nostalgia: It probably won't change your life but it's a solid 20 hours of game for not too bad a price tag.
Never experienced the first Dead Space game, but it feels like a very faithful remake of the original. Would recommend.

I have not played the original Dead Space, so I do not have a point of reference there, but in itself the game is great, looks and sounds awesome, the movement/action feels natural, and overall unmatched atmosphere.
Do not expect much story-wise imo, it's pretty standard with plenty of cliches, and visual temporal artifacts are clearly visible whatever you do.
But aside from that no bad akes, good game.

I'm not to keen on remakes of classics generally, but this one is unbelievably cool. The vibe of the original is completely intact, but everything looks so much better. 

Runs great on Linux as well. Be sure to check protonDB for some launch options and cap your framerate if you encounter stuttering. After 10 minutes of tinkering I've got buttery smooth 165 fps at a mix of high and ultra settings at 2K. 

9/10 Would highly recommend, especially if you've enjoyed the original.
